immutable_core.semantic_definition: A one-row-per-ICU-stay or ICU-semantic stay-equivalent duration summary retaining elapsed days from the current ICU discharge boundary to the first later ICU admission observed for the same subject in the captured source.
immutable_core.semantic_grain: one row per anchor-qualified summary target
immutable_core.same_name_cross_database_rule: Any database asset published as std_days_to_next_icu_admission must preserve ICU-stay or ICU-semantic stay-equivalent grain, ICU discharge anchoring, first-later ICU admission selection, source-observed follow-up scope, and explicit status semantics. Hospital admission readmission timing, MC-inclusive ICU/MCU timing, binary readmission flags, and all-system utilization claims require separate identities or separate approval.

summary_contract.window_start_rule: start at the current ICU discharge or ICU-semantic local stay-end boundary
summary_contract.window_end_rule: end at the first later ICU admission or ICU-semantic local stay-start boundary observed for the same subject
summary_contract.qualifying_row_rule: one retained row per ICU stay or ICU-semantic stay-equivalent row with subject_id, stay_id, and ICU discharge boundary present or explicitly unresolved
summary_contract.selection_rule: within the same subject, select the first later ICU stay whose admission/start time is strictly after the current ICU discharge/end time
summary_contract.tie_break_rule: order candidate future ICU admissions by admission/start time and ICU stay identifier after enforcing strict post-discharge timing
summary_contract.no_source_row_action: do not synthesize ICU stay rows outside the source ICU admission table; retain a null value for source ICU stays with no later ICU admission observed
summary_contract.partial_window_action: retain an explicit unresolved status when the current ICU discharge boundary is missing; do not reinterpret missing future events as zero duration

canonical_representation.range_role_note: The retained value is semantically nonnegative. Long observed gaps can occur because this is an observed future ICU-utilization duration inside the captured source; the plausible band is a review cue, not an exclusion rule.

null_semantics.null_meaning: No later ICU admission was observed after current ICU discharge in the captured source, or the current ICU discharge boundary was unresolved under a database-specific status.
null_does_not_mean: the patient was proven never to require future ICU care
the patient had zero days to next ICU admission
the patient was followed in all external hospital systems
the row is a hospital readmission measure
null_semantics.status_column_policy: A status column is required because null retained values need to distinguish no later observed ICU admission from unresolved missing current ICU discharge boundaries when such missing boundaries exist.

external_alignment.alignment_policy: Use external outcome and utilization terminology as semantic context only; govern this variable primarily by ICU admission/discharge boundaries, future ICU admission search rule, ICU-stay grain, and day-unit representation.
external_alignment.note: This is a source-observed ICU utilization duration, not a direct coded clinical event and not a binary readmission indicator.

new_variable_trigger_examples: changing from ICU-stay grain to hospital-admission encounter grain
changing from ICU-only timing to ICU/MCU-inclusive timing
changing from time-to-next ICU admission to a binary ICU readmission flag
changing from first later ICU admission after discharge to same-hospitalization return only
claiming all-system ICU follow-up rather than source-observed follow-up inside the captured database
version_bump_trigger_examples: adding a new reviewed database mapping while preserving the same ICU discharge anchor and future-ICU search rule
adding or refining status columns for a database with incomplete discharge boundaries while keeping retained duration semantics

allowed_database_specific_variation: local identifiers used to normalize patient and ICU stay keys
database-specific names for ICU admission and ICU discharge timestamps or offsets
database-specific evidence proving whether future ICU admissions are source-observed only or more complete within a health system
database-specific status handling when discharge timestamps are missing, if explicitly mapped
disallowed_same_name_variation: publishing hospital readmission timing as std_days_to_next_icu_admission
publishing MC-inclusive ICU/MCU timing as this ICU-only identity
publishing a binary readmission indicator as this duration variable
treating null retained values as zero days
claiming complete all-system future ICU follow-up without database-specific proof

execution_governance.formal_output_rule: Formal std_days_to_next_icu_admission outputs should go through execution.py reading this variable_spec and an approved mapping_spec. Execute-mode runtime evidence should record both spec hashes and formal output-file signatures.
execution_governance.future_non_bypassable_rule: Formal std_days_to_next_icu_admission outputs should be generated only through the governed execution.py path reading this variable_spec and an approved mapping_spec.

not_yet_approved_database_notes: AmsterdamUMCdb-1.0.2 MC-only rows are excluded from this same-name ICU identity and remain covered by the split std_days_to_next_icu_mcu_admission identity.
AmsterdamUMCdb-1.0.2 remains not approved for std_days_to_next_hospital_admission because a true hospital-admission encounter bridge is not proven.
